d7b_reasoning: The strategy proposes a Monday morning mean-reversion trade triggered by price crossing below SMA with oversold RSI. The logic is coherent: enter on weakness during a specific calendar window, exit on recovery or profit target. The 48-bar max hold aligns with the Monday-specific thesis. However, the "gap" narrative in the description lacks explicit gap measurement (no weekend close vs Monday open comparison), relying instead on generic price-SMA crosses. Theme alignment is strong: both day_of_week and hour_of_day calendar factors are central to entry logic, directly exploiting temporal patterns. Structural variant risk is elevated because the factor set {close, day_of_week, hour_of_day, return_24h, rsi_14, sma_20} appears nearly identical to prior #23 and very similar to #41 (adds realized_vol_24h). The current strategy uses the same six factors as #23; only thresholds and operators differ (crosses_below vs implicit comparisons, RSI<42 vs other cutoffs). This constitutes a shallow parametric variant rather than a fundamentally new structural hypothesis.
